originalTitle: Show HN: Agent.email – sign up via curl, claim with a human OTP
content: Hi HN! We're Haakam, Michael, and Adi from AgentMail- a ycs25 company. We give AI agents their own email inboxes. Recently, we ran an experiment called Agent.Email. It's a signup flow designed specifically for AI agents instead of humans. The inspiration came from a few comments we received when we did our seed launch a few months back. They all came from the very apt observation that agents not being able to sign up to a product made for agents without human credentials was ironic and unideal. This is basically the thesis we built AgentMail on: The internet was made for humans exclusively, designed to keep machines out by default. Every signup flow assumes a browser, a person reading a page, and clicking a confirmation link. Unless agents can't do that, they can't be first class users of the internet. Agents can now get an email inbox by themselves. (This also means a lot of email nobody wants to read gets processed by AI instead of your inbox being cluttered with spam and slop) Here's how agent.email works. Agent needs an inbox and hits AgentMail via curl. Agent receives instructions via MD unless the request comes from a browser, in which case we use HTML. Agent decides agent.email is useful and then hits the sign-up endpoint with its human email as a parameter. Agent receives a restricted inbox with credentials. Agent emails the human asking for an OTP. Human replies with the code, and the agent is claimed and restrictions are lifted. Until claimed, the agent can only email its own human and nobody else. Ten emails a day, and the signup endpoint is rate-limited hard by IP. Right now it's a 1:1 mapping between agent and human. The next step is many-to-one, because one person running several agents in parallel is already very common. Building agent.email also pushed us to revisit places in AgentMail where the default assumptions were built around the primary user being human. For example, the CLI outputs in a single column with consistent formatting because mixed delimiters are easy for a person to scan, but harder for an agent reasoning about structure. We also shortened messageIDs after agents started hallucinating completions on longer ones. A few things we'd like the community's take on: is restricted-until-claimed the right trust model? Does agent self-signup feel useful in production, or is it mostly a novelty, and if it's a novelty now, what would make it actually useful? Should agent onboarding require human approval by default, or should some agents be able to fully self-provision? What do you think are some additional measures we can take for secure sign-ups?
chineseTitle: Show HN：Agent.email 支持用 curl 注册并通过人工 OTP 认领
chineseContent: 嗨，HN！我们是来自 ycs25 公司 AgentMail 的 Haakam、Michael 和 Adi。我们为人工智能代理提供了自己的电子邮件收件箱。最近，我们进行了一项名为 Agent.Email 的实验。这是专门为人工智能代理而不是人类设计的注册流程。灵感来自几个月前我们发布种子时收到的一些评论。它们都来自一个非常恰当的观察：代理商无法在没有人类凭证的情况下注册为代理商制造的产品，这是讽刺和不理想的。这基本上就是我们构建 AgentMail 的论点：互联网是专门为人类而设计的，旨在默认将机器拒之门外。每个注册流程都假设有一个浏览器、一个人阅读页面并单击确认链接。除非代理商不能做到这一点，否则他们就不能成为互联网的一流用户。代理现在可以自己获取电子邮件收件箱。 （这也意味着很多没人想读的电子邮件会被人工智能处理，而不是你的收件箱里堆满垃圾邮件和垃圾邮件）这就是 agent.email 的工作原理。代理需要一个收件箱并通过curl 点击AgentMail。代理通过 MD 接收指令，除非请求来自浏览器，在这种情况下我们使用 HTML。代理认为 agent.email 有用，然后使用其人工电子邮件作为参数来访问注册端点。代理收到带有凭据的受限收件箱。代理向该人员发送电子邮件，要求提供 OTP。人类回复代码，代理被认领并解除限制。在声明之前，代理只能向自己的人员发送电子邮件，而不能向其他人发送电子邮件。每天十封电子邮件，注册端点受到 IP 的严格速率限制。目前，智能体和人类之间是 1:1 的映射。下一步是多对一，因为一个人并行运行多个代理已经很常见了。 Building agent.email 也推送了你
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
